headlines.text: Suspected mine spotted in Strait of Hormuz as Iran ceasefire talks stall
Hegseth: US is 'more than capable' of resuming Iran strikes if deal falls through
Trump ends Iran Situation Room meeting without announcing 'final determination'
Dow closes above 51,000 for first time ever; S&P 500 logs 9th straight winning week
Pam Bondi blames Todd Blanche for Epstein files mess; Dems threaten subpoena of acting AG
A24's 'Backrooms' eyes $80M-$90M opening, shattering studio record on day one
Federal judge blocks Trump DOJ 'lawfare' fund temporarily as legal challenge proceeds
Gas prices near $4.43/gallon nationally — up 50% since US-Israel launched Iran war
India and US sign critical minerals and rare earths framework deal in New Delhi
14-year-old Shrey Parikh wins 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee with 32-word lightning round

breaking.text: Suspected floating mine reported in Strait of Hormuz by Omani authorities — shipping alerts issued

stories.hed: Mine Scare in Strait of Hormuz as Trump Exits Situation Room Without a Deal
stories.dek: Oman spotted a suspected floating mine in the world's most important oil chokepoint, which is a great way to remind everyone why this war keeps costing $4.43 a gallon. Trump held a 'final determination' meeting on the Iran ceasefire extension, then walked out without determining anything final.

stories.hed: Russian Drone Strikes NATO Member Romania; EU Drafts New Sanctions Package
stories.dek: A Russian drone struck an apartment block in Romania — a full NATO member state — escalating pressure on the alliance as the EU scrambles to finalize a new round of sanctions against Moscow.

stories.hed: Bondi Fingers Blanche in Epstein Files Fiasco; Democrats Eye Subpoena of Acting AG
stories.dek: Former AG Pam Bondi told House Oversight she 'delegated' the Epstein files disaster entirely to acting AG Todd Blanche, who also happens to be Trump's former personal attorney. Democrats say that's convenient and are now lining up a subpoena, assuming Republicans let them.

stories.hed: Federal Judge Declines to Block Trump Mail-In Voting Executive Order
stories.dek: A federal judge in Washington, D.C., refused to temporarily block Trump's executive order restricting mail-in voting, keeping the order in effect as further legal challenges work their way through the courts.

stories.hed: Dell Surges 33% in One Day While Your Gas Costs More Than Your Lunch
stories.dek: Dell Technologies had its best single trading day ever — up nearly 33% on AI server demand — the same week the national gas average hit $4.43. The stock market is having a historic bull run fueled by AI optimism while the average American is doing math at the pump. Two economies, one country, zero irony.

markets.context: All three indexes closed at record highs Friday on Iran ceasefire optimism and a Dell-led AI tech surge, capping the S&P 500's ninth consecutive winning week.

gas.note: Up 50% since the Iran war started — your car is now a financial commitment.

popCulture.hed: A24's 'Backrooms' Opens to Potential $80M-$90M Weekend, Crushing Studio Record
popCulture.dek: Twenty-year-old YouTube director Kane Parsons just turned a $10M internet creepypasta horror film into A24's biggest opening ever, with Friday estimates hitting $38M alone. 'Backrooms' is outpacing 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' in its second weekend, which is deeply humbling for a galaxy far, far away.

genZ.definition: TikTok creators are building absurd fantasy squads for the upcoming FIFA 2026 World Cup by drafting not just footballers but politicians, memes, inanimate objects, and random pop culture figures onto national teams. It went viral this week as the tournament approaches and everyone has opinions about who belongs on Bosnia's roster.
genZ.usage: Iran nuclear negotiators are playing 4-4-2 with Hegseth in goal, Trump on Truth Social as the striker, and a suspected floating mine as the sweeper.

tldr: Trump left his Iran ceasefire 'final determination' meeting without determining anything final, a suspected mine popped up in the Strait of Hormuz, and markets hit record highs anyway because the stock market has decided vibes are more important than mines. Meanwhile, a 20-year-old YouTuber's $10M horror film about fluorescent hallways is on track to out-gross Star Wars this weekend, which about sums up 2026.
